mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
regime_hint: any regime with declining employment trend
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, signaling economic strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 700,291 million and HOUST at 1,460 thousand as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST are rising month-over-month, or joint Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 3-month moving average, it signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when INDPRO falls below 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, eroding bond returns and driving equity risk premium compression that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 309.685 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.5% and FEDFUNDS < 4.5%, or joint Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, it signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, allowing the Federal Reserve to pause or cut rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations expand.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening with policy pivot expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, it signals demand exhaustion and recessionary risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS MoM change ≤ 0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0% and RSAFS is flat/declining, or joint Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously expands, it signals robust supply-side capacity expansion and labor productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through improved profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.5739 and PAYEMS at 157,700 thousand as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized supply and labor growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 62% of instances when INDPRO MoM > 1.5% and PAYEMS is rising, or joint Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
